angle: A red-team eval set to MEASURE the false-relevance rate: add an off-corpus query class (kubernetes-style terms with expected gap/weak) wired into the eval harness with a false-confirm-rate metric and a CI threshold.

A red-team eval set to MEASURE the false-relevance rate: add an off-corpus query class (kubernetes-style terms with expected gap/weak) wired into the eval harness with a false-confirm-rate metric and a CI threshold.
title: Add an off_corpus red-team query class to the golden set (the missing fixture, not the missing metric)
problem: The false-confirm METRIC primitive already exists and is even partially wired, but the FIXTURE it needs does not. ground-truth.json has a `hard_negative` category (lib/eval/ground-truth-data.ts:23, 6 queries id 92-97), but those are IR-distractor queries that all carry a relevance-3 TRUE target (verified by reading data/ground-truth.json: every hard_negative query has exactly one relevance:3 row). They are not off-corpus no-target terms. The two terms the 029 benchmark proved false-confirm — `kubernetes` (0.78 good on the unrelated Spec Memory Runtime Retention Cleanup doc) and `authentication` (0.50 weak, correct) — are absent from the corpus entirely (benchmark-results.md:71-72,81). So buildGroundTruthLabelViews (eval-metrics.ts:724) can never produce an off-corpus sample: it keys `expectedCitable = !isHardNegative && maxRelevance >= threshold` (line 758) off graded relevances that only exist for queries WITH a target. There is no query in the harness whose CORRECT answer is gap/weak-because-nothing-relevant-exists.
evidence: lib/eval/ground-truth-data.ts:18-30 (QueryCategory union, hard_negative present, no off_corpus); data/ground-truth.json (all 6 hard_negative queries 92-97 carry a relevance:3 row — verified by node inspection); eval-metrics.ts:743,758 (expectedCitable derived from relevance, no off-corpus path); benchmark-results.md:71-72,81 (kubernetes 0.78 good false-confirm, authentication 0.50 weak, both absent from golden set).
proposal: Add a new `off_corpus` QueryCategory to lib/eval/ground-truth-data.ts (line 18-30 union) and seed 6-10 off-corpus red-team queries in data/ground-truth.json modelled on the benchmark's `kubernetes`/`authentication` rows: single-token or short technical terms for concepts genuinely absent from this spec corpus (kubernetes, oauth, kafka, graphql, terraform, webpack, etc.), each with ZERO relevance rows (so maxRelevance=0). Extend buildGroundTruthLabelViews so an off_corpus query sets expectedCitable=false (it already does via the maxRelevance>=threshold clause, but add an explicit `isOffCorpus`/treat-as-negative flag so the class is distinguishable from a hard_negative that has a target). Keep `kubernetes` as a permanent regression anchor since the benchmark pinned its exact false-confirm.
benefit: Creates the only thing standing between the existing false-confirm machinery and a real measurement: a query class whose ground-truth-correct verdict is gap/weak. Makes the 029 finding reproducible on demand instead of a one-off 144-cell manual run, and pins kubernetes as a regression guard against any future calibration drift.
risk: Choosing terms that are actually off-corpus requires care — a term assumed absent might have a weak true match in the live corpus, mislabelling it. Mitigate by selecting terms from unrelated tech domains (container orchestration, web frameworks) the spec-kit corpus provably never discusses, and by snapshotting the top hit at authoring time the way the benchmark did.

angle: A red-team eval set to MEASURE the false-relevance rate: add an off-corpus query class (kubernetes-style terms with expected gap/weak) wired into the eval harness with a false-confirm-rate metric and a CI threshold.
title: Wire a false-confirm-rate driver that runs assessRequestQuality on the off_corpus class and reports falseGoodOnHardNegatives
problem: computeCitabilityConfusionMetrics (eval-metrics.ts:885) already computes exactly the metric the angle asks for — it extends the gate confusion matrix with `hardNegativeCount` and `falseGoodOnHardNegatives` (lines 889-901), i.e. how often `predicted==='good'` while `expectedCitable===false`. But grep confirms NO eval driver under scripts/evals/ calls it, nor computeGateVerdictMetrics, nor assessRequestQuality — only the ablation-framework and two vitest files reference them. The one live end-to-end path, ablation-framework.ts buildCorpusMetrics (line 1119), runs assessRequestQuality on real rows (line 1001) and builds gateSamples (line 1124-1129) but feeds the WEAKER computeGateVerdictMetrics (line 1156), dropping the per-hard-negative breakdown, and it only fires inside a full ablation study (partition 'citability-confusion', line 1114) — there is no standalone, fast, reportable false-confirm number. The benchmark had to measure this by hand across 4 models because the harness exposes no such driver.
evidence: eval-metrics.ts:885-903 (computeCitabilityConfusionMetrics with falseGoodOnHardNegatives); grep: no scripts/evals/*.mjs references computeCitabilityConfusionMetrics/computeGateVerdictMetrics/assessRequestQuality; ablation-framework.ts:1001,1124-1129,1156 (live gate path uses the weaker computeGateVerdictMetrics inside an ablation study only); benchmark-results.md:14,81 (the false-confirm was measured manually across 144 cells).
proposal: Add scripts/evals/run-false-confirm-eval.mjs following the established copy-DB driver pattern (backupSqlite + readActiveEmbedder + prepareEvalDatabase, identical to run-calibration-eval.mjs:42-94). For each off_corpus query: run hybridSearchEnhanced (evaluationMode), call confidenceScoring.computeResultConfidence + assessRequestQuality to get the live `predicted` label, build {predicted, expectedCitable:false, isHardNegative:true} samples via buildGroundTruthLabelViews, and emit computeCitabilityConfusionMetrics. Report falseConfirmRate = falseGoodOnHardNegatives / hardNegativeCount plus the per-query top-hit + score (so a regression names the doc, as the benchmark did for kubernetes). Output JSON to /tmp like the siblings.
benefit: Turns the dormant confusion-metric into a one-command, deterministic false-confirm number reusable across embedder/calibration changes, replacing the 144-cell manual benchmark. Surfaces the exact spurious doc per failing term for root-cause, not just an aggregate rate.
risk: The driver couples to the live embedder shard and the absolute-relevance calibration default; a different active embedder will shift scores and could flip kubernetes' verdict, making the baseline embedder-specific. Mitigate by recording the active embedder in the output (run-calibration-eval.mjs already reads it) and treating the rate as embedder-scoped.

angle: A red-team eval set to MEASURE the false-relevance rate: add an off-corpus query class (kubernetes-style terms with expected gap/weak) wired into the eval harness with a false-confirm-rate metric and a CI threshold.
title: Add a false-confirm-rate THRESHOLD that fails the eval, mirroring the existing measurability gates
problem: There is no threshold anywhere that fails a run when the gate false-confirms an off-corpus term — grep for falseGood/false_confirm across mcp_server returns only the metric definition and one unit test, never a gate. The harness already has the PATTERN for threshold gates: run-eval-v2.mjs frames itself as 'the TRACK B measurability gate' (line 3) with a first-class fidelity delta (lines 21-24, 317-318), and the run-eval drivers exit non-zero on failure (run-eval-v2.mjs:356-357). But the false-confirm dimension has no acceptance bar, so even after the metric is surfaced, a calibration regression that pushes more off-corpus terms to good would print a worse number and still pass green. The 029 benchmark observed the 0.78 false-confirm but nothing in the repo would have CAUGHT it.
evidence: grep: falseGood/false_confirm only in eval-metrics.ts + tests, no gate; run-eval-v2.mjs:3,21-24,356-359 (existing measurability-gate + non-zero-exit pattern); eval-calibration-ab.vitest.ts:222-228 (existing assertion shape for falseGoodOnHardNegatives); benchmark-results.md:81 (kubernetes 0.78 good went uncaught by any automated gate).
proposal: Add SPECKIT_FALSE_CONFIRM_MAX_RATE (default 0.0 for the off_corpus class — no off-corpus term may earn good) to the new driver, and exit non-zero when falseConfirmRate exceeds it, matching run-eval-v2.mjs's CLI-gate exit convention (line 356-359). Pin kubernetes as an explicit must-not-be-good assertion so its known false-confirm becomes a tracked, named regression rather than a silent number. Optionally also add a vitest that drives assessRequestQuality on the off_corpus golden rows and asserts the rate against the same env bar, so CI guards it without a live DB (the eval-calibration-ab.vitest.ts:222-228 pattern already asserts falseGoodOnHardNegatives).
benefit: Converts the measurement into an enforceable contract: a calibration change that worsens off-corpus false-confirm fails fast with the offending term named, instead of requiring a 144-cell manual re-run to notice. Gives the 005 program a concrete pass/fail acceptance bar for the calibration-fix work the 030 research is scoping.
risk: Setting the threshold to 0.0 while kubernetes currently scores 0.78 good means the gate starts RED on day one — it documents an open bug rather than passing. That is intended (it is the bug the program exists to fix), but it must be introduced as a known-failing/quarantined gate or an xfail until the calibration re-fit lands, or it will block unrelated CI. Coordinate the threshold flip with the calibration fix.

angle: A red-team eval set to MEASURE the false-relevance rate: add an off-corpus query class (kubernetes-style terms with expected gap/weak) wired into the eval harness with a false-confirm-rate metric and a CI threshold.
title: Promote the off_corpus class into run-eval-v2 / run-retrieval-flag-eval class lists so it is not silently skipped
problem: Both standing drivers hard-enumerate the query classes they cover and explicitly report any class present in the union but absent from their list as a coverage gap rather than silently dropping it — run-eval-v2.mjs MEASURABILITY_CLASSES freezes ['thematic_multi_target','causal_chain','hard_negative'] (lines 51-55) and reports missingClasses (line 279,331); run-retrieval-flag-eval.mjs CLASS_DIMENSIONS.category lists the seven categories (lines 27-36) and notes 'any class enumerated here but absent from the loaded golden set is reported as a coverage gap' (lines 24-26). A newly-added off_corpus category would be invisible to these drivers — neither lists it — so the red-team class would exist in the data but never be split out or coverage-checked by the two main drivers, only by the new standalone driver.
evidence: run-eval-v2.mjs:51-55,221-222,279,331 (frozen MEASURABILITY_CLASSES, empty-ground-truth skip, missingClasses report); run-retrieval-flag-eval.mjs:24-36 (CLASS_DIMENSIONS.category list + coverage-gap-on-absence comment); lib/eval/ground-truth-data.ts:18-30 (category union the drivers mirror).
proposal: Add 'off_corpus' to run-retrieval-flag-eval.mjs CLASS_DIMENSIONS.category (line 28-36) so its recall/MRR split surfaces the class, and decide deliberately whether to add it to run-eval-v2.mjs MEASURABILITY_CLASSES — likely NOT, since eval-v2 measures completeRecall@K (off-corpus queries have no targets to recall, so they belong on the false-confirm driver, not the recall gate). Document the routing decision in a comment so a future reader knows off_corpus is intentionally a citability fixture, not a recall fixture. This keeps the coverage-gap reporting honest.
benefit: Prevents the red-team class from becoming a silently-unmeasured corner: the retrieval-flag driver will report off-corpus recall behavior (it should be near-zero, a useful sanity check) and the eval-v2 coverage report won't misclassify it. Makes the class's purpose explicit so it isn't later 'fixed' by adding spurious relevance rows.
risk: Adding off_corpus to a recall driver that expects targets could produce confusing zero-recall rows or, if mishandled, divide-by-zero on an empty ground-truth set; run-eval-v2's meanCompleteRecallProfile already skips queries with empty ground truth (line 221-222), so the safe move is to keep off_corpus OFF the recall gate and only on the citability/false-confirm path. Low blast radius — these are read-only driver class lists.
